threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You keep using the bus app for everything, even when the stop's just around the corner. Locals know to save their phone battery for the unexpected detour.
Caught a night bus from Brixton and the driver was blasting old reggae. Made the 3am ride feel like a mini party, even with half-asleep passengers.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a family having a picnic on the roof of an abandoned warehouse, kids playing next to graffiti, I knew this place feels alive.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Pankow, it’s perfectly normal to see an old man biking to the bakery in full suit and bow tie—just for that fresh Brötchen. Only in Berlin.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a true local by their disdain for the new vegan taco place. We all know La Taqueria is where it’s at, no contest.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus today where an artist was sketching the driver. That's classic SF—creativity thriving in transit!

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the nightlife, but it’s the quiet mornings at Plaça del Sol that make the neighborhood feel like home.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell they’re new when they still look confused at the metro maps and ask locals for directions instead of using Google Maps.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it the Overground instead of just 'the train'. Locals know it’s more than just a colour on the map.
Saw a guy in a full tuxedo on the 35, debating whether to go home or crash a wedding. Only in Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The first time I heard a spontaneous jam session from my balcony, with people shouting ‘Berliner Luft’ across the street. That’s when I felt home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Schöneberg, you'll catch the faintest sound of spontaneous opera coming from the park on warm evenings—locals just belt it out, no stage needed.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Locals know to avoid 16th and Mission at 5 PM; it’s the real-life game of Frogger with those buses and bike lanes.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tuxedo, top hat and all, riding the 14. He chatted with a lady in sweatpants about the best taco spots. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think Gràcia is just a party zone. They miss the quiet streets where locals still gossip over afternoon vermut.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their bewilderment at the bus routes—especially when they try to navigate without the TMB app.
